_comment: operation-router v2.4.6 config. Routing and model assignments are unchanged from v2.4.5. Verified 2026-07-22 against codex-cli 0.144.5 models_cache: gpt-5.6-sol/terra/luna are exposed. Claude and Grok CLI capabilities remain as verified 2026-07-20: claude 2.1.212, grok 0.2.102. Do not hand-edit model IDs without re-verifying.

headlessPermissions._comment: v2.3.5 retains the v2.3.3 headless permission policy unchanged. grok --allow/--deny are compat aliases for Claude Code --allowedTools/--disallowedTools. mode=alwaysApprove maps to --always-approve: grok's built-in policy classifies heredoc/command-substitution commands as ask rules, and under the retired dontAsk mode a headless run cannot answer, so the whole session dies with cancellationCategory=PermissionCancelled (measured 2026-07-20, op2-issue3 debug log). --always-approve removes ask prompts while --deny rules still take priority and block matched commands as a graceful tool error (probe-verified). Trade-off: non-terminal tools are no longer constrained by the allow list; deny remains the effective guard.
headlessPermissions._deny_note: First-line blocklist only. Pattern matching is brittle and covers the Grok worker only. Test-RepoBoundaryViolation is a post-execution integrity check for selected critical files; it is not an OS sandbox and does not detect unwatched access, reads, creation, or exfiltration.

1._effort_note: v2.4.0: medium→high. Operation 1 is high-risk and every other op1 slot (grok/sol/opus) is high; this was the sole outlier. Implementer↔reviewer separation kept (Opus end-reviews).

execution._comment: The worker host limits only foreground wait. Active raw artifacts are sanitized at terminal finalization. Retention always protects every latest execution receipt reference and applies the count only to unreferenced terminal generations. A timeout never starts a second worker.

quotaUnknownPatterns: you have exceeded your current quota
